post_rendered:
Welcome to Mafia Game 161: Yes Minister Mafia
Rules
  • Rules
    • 1.1: Read the rules. All of them. Read them multiple times if necessary.
    • 1.2: Do not post at night.
    • 1.3: You may PM me at any time and ask me anything, such as rule clarification.
    • 1.4: Do not quote or pretend to quote any PMs from me, including role PMs.
    • 1.5: Do not talk about this game outside of this topic to anyone other than the mods in charge, unless I specifically allow you to do so.
    • 1.6: You may not use any formatting that has an effect of concealing text. This includes size=1, hide="", color=#FFFFFF, and color=white, among others. This does not include adding extra spaces.
    • 1.7: Do not edit or delete posts you have already submitted; this includes editing right after you post (what goes in the thread stays in the thread). If you for any reason find the need to change your post, use EBWOP or EBWON as necessary.
    • 1.8: If you are dead, do not post anything else about the game anywhere. You may still spectate the game, but you may no longer post in this thread.
    • 1.19: Do not use the colors red (#FF0000), blue (#0000FF), green (#008000), or anything that appears similar, as these colors are used for modding purposes.
    • 1.10: Confirm by posting Confirm
  • Voting/Lynches
    • 2.1: Post votes in bold font in the format "Vote: <insert player here>". Since AoPS does not distinguish between usernames with different capitalizations, the capitalization of the player name does not matter. For example, "Vote: fdas" and Vote: fDAs is an acceptable vote, while "Vote: fdas", "Vote fdas", “vote: fdas, Vote:fdas, Vote: fads, Vote: fdsa, and "Vote: fdas" are not.
    • 2.2: Votes may not be placed in hide tags.
    • 2.3: Improperly made votes will not be counted under any circumstances. It does not matter if it is the end of the game hammer and everyone revealed their roles. Hint: don’t reveal your roles and declare victory until I made the official end of the game post.
    • 2.4: You may vote for no lynch to occur during a day. Vote this by including in your post "Vote: No lynch".
    • 2.5: You do not have to unvote before making a new vote, though it is appreciated if you do so. Unvote in the format "Unvote". An unvote will undo your previous vote, if any.
    • 2.6: Spell the username of the player you wish to vote for exactly. Do not use nicknames.
    • 2.7: If at any point in a day more than half of the votes are for a single player, that player will be lynched. It will then immediately be twilight.
    • 2.8: If at any point in a day at least half of the votes are for no lynch, then there will be no lynch. It will then be twilight.
    • 2.9: The moment the deadline passes, no more votes will be counted even if the mod has not made a post.
    • 2.10: During twilight, players may still post. However, no more votes and/or unvotes will be processed.
    • 2.11: Once I post a end-of-day scene, the person lynched (if any) is officially dead. Night then immediately begins. You may not post during night.

  • Activity
    • 3.1: Each day will be 336 hours (2 weeks). The moderator may extend the deadline for the end of the day at his/her discretion. Note that this will be unlikely.
    • 3.2: The deadlines will follow the official time
    • 3.3: The deadline will occur the very second 336 hours has occurred.
    • 3.4: If the deadline is met and no person is lynched, it will be considered no lynch and will proceed to be twilight.
    • 3.5: The night deadline will be 72 hours after night begins.
    • 3.6: When the night deadline is met, if the moderator does not receive a night action from either a player or his/her partner(s) (if you have them), the player will lose the chance to perform an action during that night.
    • 3.7: Any night results will be sent after the night deadline
    • 3.8: Night will end at some time after the night deadline when the mod makes the post announcing the end of night.
    • 3.9: If a player does not post in a 48-hour period and he/she is not in V/LA, you will receive an in-game prod.
    • 3.10: If a player does not post in another 24-hour period after receiving an in-game prod and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will receive a PM prod.
    • 3.11: If a player does not post in another 48-hour period after receiving a PM prod and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will be replaced, unless you post before the moderator finds a replacement.
    • 3.12: If a player receives 5 PM Prods, he/she will be replaced
    • 3.13: If a player is going to be V/LA, he/she must post in bold in-thread or PM me. The moderator will not prod the player unless the V/LA is too long.

  • Etiquette
    • 4.1: Do not use inappropriate language.
    • 4.2: Do not make any personal attacks.
    • 4.3: Follow all of the rules.
    • 4.4: Be mature.
    • 4.5: For a minor rule infraction, newbies can receive up to two warnings, SE’s up to one, and IC’s none. If a player commits an infraction after he/she has run out of warnings, or commits a modkill level infraction, he/she will be modkilled. Please do not make me modkill you.

  • Moderators/Co-Moderators
    • 5.1: I (fdas) am the moderator of Mafia Game 161 and etr217 will be your co-mod
    • 5.2: Red-colored text (#FF0000) is used for official votecounts and activity checks by the moderators. It is also used for mafia flavor-related posts.
    • 5.3: Blue-colored text (#0000FF) is used for the moderator and co-mod's posts other than votecounts and activity checks.
    • 5.4: Green-colored text (#00FF00) is used for town flavor-related posts.
    • 5.5: The not voting section is to be ignored if it contradicts any other part of the votecount
    • 5.6: If an error to the votecount is not pointed out within 5 days, the incorrect votecount will become official.
    • 5.7: The moderator may change the rules at any time.
    • 5.8: The moderator has the last say in interpreting the rules.

  • Read all of the rules

Setup
Roles
Arnold Robinson
Humphrey Appleby
Ordinary Civil Servant
Russian Spy
Jim Hacker
Anne Hacker
Bernard Woolley
Dorothy Wainwright
5 Cabinet Ministers


You are Sir Arnold Robinson Knight Grand Cross of the Most Distinguished Order of Saint Michael and Saint George, Commander of the Royal Victorian Order, Master of the Arts (Oxon). You are retired member of the civil service. As a long serving member of the civil service your character is beyond reproach (Investigation immune). Every night you may discuss with the fellow members of the civil service to manipulate events so that someone is removed from office. You win when the only players remaining are civil servants (Mafia) or it is impossible for this not to happen.

You are Sir Humphrey Appleby, Knight Grand Cross of the Most Honourable Order of the Bath, Knight Commander of the Most Excellent Order of the British Empire, Member of the Royal Victorian Order, Master of the Arts (Oxon). You are the Cabinet Secretary. You are a master of obfuscation and manipulation, every night you may target someone who will then be unable to perform any actions. If you target the Prime Minister you will steal both of his votes. Every night you may discuss with the fellow members of the civil service to manipulate events so that someone is removed from office. You win when the only players remaining are civil servants (Mafia) or it is impossible for this not to happen.

You are an Ordinary Civil servant. Every night you may discuss with the fellow members of the civil service to manipulate events so that someone is removed from office. You win when the only players remaining are civil servants (Mafia) or it is impossible for this not to happen.

You are a Russian spy. Every night you may kill someone. Due to your blackmail material you can save yourself from being removed from office once (1 shot BP). You win when you are the only player left or it is impossible for this not to happen.

You are The Right Honorable James George Hacker, Baron Hacker of Islington, Knight of the Most Noble Order of the Garter, Honorary Doctor of Law (Oxon). You are the Prime minister. You vote counts as two votes. Every night you may talk to your wife Anne Hacker () here. You win when there are only government aligned players (Town) left in the game.

You are Anne Hacker. You are the wife of Jim Hacker () and you can talk to him every night here. You win when there are only government aligned players (Town) left in the game.

You are Sir Bernard Woolley, Knight Grand Cross of the Most Honourable Order of the Bath, Master of the Arts (Oxon). You are the Principal Private Secretary of the Prime Minister. Every night you may choose someone to protect. If that person is targeted by the civil service, you will foil their plans to remove him from office. If the person is targeted by the Russian spy, you will die in his place. You win when there are only government aligned players (Town) left in the game.

You are Dorothy Wainwright. You are the Chief Political Advisor to the Prime Minister. Every night you may investigate someone to determine their alignment. You win when there are only government aligned players (Town) left in the game.

You are a Cabinet Minister. You are part of the government. You have no tool except your vote. You win when there are only government aligned players (Town) left in the game.

post_rendered:
I think an analogy is needed:
The world is divided into two groups: Remocrats and Depublicans. They each have very different views and opinions, and are at war.
Gal McBama and Eorge O'Cain both live in Remocra. However, Gal is a Depublican spy, trying to collect vital info for Depublica's war effort.

Gal has befriended Eorge (a normal Remocran citizen), and are talking one day:
GAL: And they were roommates.
EORGE: (laughing)
GAL: I like that laugh. heur heur heur
EORGE: Hey man, watch out. Vines are a common thing to reference in Depublica. Just be careful; I wouldn't want you to get caught.
(Some other people overhear this, and give Gal suspicious looks).
GAL: heh heh heh

You see, Gal has two things to do in this situation.
OPTION ONE: Gal immediately stops referencing vines, to stop doing suspicious things. However, a sudden drop off in his natural behavior to avoid suspicion gets him looked at by everybody more, which leads him to do
OPTION TWO: Instead of doing what people would expect Depublican spies to do, Gal continues with his vine references. However, this is suspicious behavior. This option two is also the alternative to option one, which is blatantly spy-ish while this is not. So this is what all the Depublican spies would do, which makes it then more suspicious, leading us back to
OPTION ONE: Stop all suspicious vine references, and avoid the spy-expected option two. However, this then becomes the spy ridden option, leading us to
OPTOIN TWO: This is a paradox.

I believe this speaks for itself.

post_canonical:
I think an analogy is needed:
The world is divided into two groups: Remocrats and Depublicans. They each have very different views and opinions, and are at war.
Gal McBama and Eorge O'Cain both live in Remocra. However, Gal is a Depublican spy, trying to collect vital info for Depublica's war effort. 

Gal has befriended Eorge (a normal Remocran citizen), and are talking one day:
GAL: And they were roommates.
EORGE: (laughing)
GAL: I like that laugh. heur heur heur
EORGE: Hey man, watch out. Vines are a common thing to reference in Depublica. Just be careful; I wouldn't want you to get caught.
(Some other people overhear this, and give Gal suspicious looks).
GAL: heh heh heh

You see, Gal has two things to do in this situation.
OPTION ONE: Gal immediately stops referencing vines, to stop doing suspicious things. However, a sudden drop off in his natural behavior to avoid suspicion gets him looked at by everybody more, which leads him to do 
OPTION TWO: Instead of doing what people would expect Depublican spies to do, Gal continues with his vine references. However, this is suspicious behavior. This option two is also the alternative to option one, which is blatantly spy-ish while this is not. So this is what all the Depublican spies would do, which makes it then more suspicious, leading us back to 
OPTION ONE: Stop all suspicious vine references, and avoid the spy-expected option two. However, this then becomes the spy ridden option, leading us to 
OPTOIN TWO: This is a paradox.

I believe this speaks for itself.
